issues.body: build_userdata interpolates branch, cells derived from MPC_PARTIES, WATCHDOG_SECS, and DF_MIN_MB directly into an unquoted heredoc that becomes a root-run cloud-init script. Only MPC_ROWS is partially validated; BRANCH and MPC_PARTIES are unrestricted. For example, a branch containing a quote followed by shell syntax breaks out of `git fetch -q origin "$branch"`, while a crafted MPC_PARTIES value is emitted directly beneath the CELLS heredoc and can terminate that heredoc. This turns ordinary launcher inputs into arbitrary root code execution on the instance. The self-test uses fixed benign inputs and cannot detect this.
issues.fix_hint: Validate every interpolated value against strict syntax and bounds (including positive integer parties/watchdogs/floor and a conservative Git ref format), or transfer structured/base64-encoded data and decode it without evaluation. Avoid embedding caller data in shell source. Add negative self-tests with quotes, newlines, heredoc terminators, and shell metacharacters and assert rejection before any AWS call.
issues.title: Untrusted arguments and environment values enable root command injection in user-data

issues.body: The generated user-data uses only `set -x`, not fail-fast error handling. Failures from apt, rustup, clone/fetch/checkout, or `cargo build` therefore do not stop execution. Each benchmark failure is converted into a cell-failed JSON, and the script still writes MPC_CEILING_DONE. The orchestrator consequently treats a broken setup/build as a completed ceiling run and pulls apparently valid metadata/results. This violates the stated safety property that a faster-but-wrong or invalid run cannot be reported, and the marker-only self-test is vacuous for this behavior.
issues.fix_hint: Make cloud-init fail closed (`set -euo pipefail` with intentional exceptions narrowly handled), write a distinct failure sentinel/status on traps, and create MPC_CEILING_DONE only after prerequisites/build succeed and the intended matrix reaches an explicitly accepted terminal state. Add a hermetic test using stubbed failing commands to prove no success sentinel is produced after clone/build failure.
issues.title: Instance script writes the completion sentinel after setup or build failures

verdict.summary: The harness has root-level command-injection paths because several caller-controlled values are interpolated verbatim into EC2 user-data, and it can also report a completed run after prerequisite/build failures because the instance script does not fail closed. These are blocking for a credentialed EC2 benchmark launcher. The hermetic self-test checks for marker strings but does not exercise either invariant.
